text.title: Terraform Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Engineer applies from laptop; main branch diverges; next CI apply errors.
antipattern.reason: No CI-only gate; engineers have direct cloud credentials.
antipattern.note: Fix: Remove laptop creds. Configure OIDC trust GitHub → cloud IAM. terraform apply only in CI.

antipattern.description: git diff shows database password in plain text inside prod.tfvars.
antipattern.reason: No Vault / Secrets Manager integration; vars added inline.
antipattern.note: Fix: Move secret to Vault. Reference via data.vault_generic_secret. Mark variable sensitive = true. Rotate the leaked credential.

antipattern.description: Two engineers run plan and see different diffs; CI produces a third result.
antipattern.note: Fix: Pin to exact version in versions.tf. Bump deliberately via PR.

antipattern.description: 5 micro-services have a 200-line ECS service block; a security fix needs to be applied 5 times.
antipattern.reason: No module abstraction; copy-paste seemed easier.
antipattern.note: Fix: Extract to module. Tag v1.0.0. Replace inline copies with module references. Bump callers to fix once.

antipattern.description: Console-edited security group survives in prod; PR diff doesn't show it; next apply silently reverts.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add weekly plan job; surface non-zero diff to #infra channel; triage in next office hours.
